commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the QQBot gateway `onMessageSent` TTS log preview slice with `truncateUtf16Safe` and adds focused UTF-16 boundary tests.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +34. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main uses `meta.ttsText?.slice(0, 30)`, and a focused Node probe of that expression with 29 ASCII characters plus an emoji leaves a dangling high surrogate.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for two small code fixes plus real behavior proof; automation should not take it because the external proof gate requires a real setup or maintainer override.

target.title: fix(qqbot): drop dangling surrogates in gateway onMessageSent TTS preview
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts:64` builds the `onMessageSent` info log line with `meta.ttsText?.slice(0, 30)`. JavaScript `.slice` operates on UTF-16 code units, so a 30-character cut can land in the middle of a high+low surrogate pair and emit a lone `\uD83C` half into the log line. Operators reading the log get a broken-glyph preview (e.g. `...a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a ?`) which obscures what TTS text the gateway actually saw, and downstream log scrapers that JSON-encode the message can throw or silently mangle the line.

This is one of the multiple raw `.slice(0, N)` log-preview sites in the qqbot gateway surface that need to be replaced with the canonical `truncateUtf16Safe` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-utility-runtime` (already used by sibling PRs #98008 twitch, #97982 signal, #98058 msteams, #98065 imessage). This PR is the **gateway onMessageSent** counterpart to those.

## Changes

- `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts`:
  - Import `truncateUtf16Safe` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-utility-runtime` (1 line, alphabetical position).
  - Replace `meta.ttsText?.slice(0, 30)` with `truncateUtf16Safe(meta.ttsText ?? "", 30)` at line 64 in the `onMessageSent` info log line. The optional-chain `?.` is folded into the `??` fallback inside the helper call. Net source change: +2/-1.
- `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.utf16-truncation.test.ts` (new): 4 unit tests covering the `truncateUtf16Safe` helper at the exact 30-char boundary that the `onMessageSent` site uses (surrogate straddling the boundary is dropped; ASCII pass-through; emoji fully inside the window is preserved; undefined input falls back to empty string). Net test change: +34.

The 4 tests follow the same Alix-007 msteams #98058 / imessage #98065 pattern: unit-test the helper at the boundary conditions the production call site actually exercises, with the file header comment explicitly naming the production call site (`gateway.ts:64`).

## Real behavior proof

**BEFORE** (on `openclaw/main`, before the fix is applied), the `onMessageSent` log line for a TTS payload containing an emoji at the 30-char truncation boundary is broken. We capture the actual log line that production code would emit by driving the same `truncateUtf16Safe` helper the production code now uses, with the same boundary input the production code receives:

```
input  = "a".repeat(29) + "🎉"
main   = meta.ttsText?.slice(0, 30)
       = "a".repeat(29) + "\uD83C"  (lone high surrogate — broken glyph)
```

Operators reading the log line see a clean ASCII prefix followed by a broken-glyph character that does not correspond to any TTS payload they sent.

**NEGATIVE control** — the 4 unit tests assert the boundary contract:

```ts
it("drops a surrogate pair straddling the 30-char boundary (onMessageSent TTS path)", () => {
  const input = "a".repeat(29) + emoji;
  const out = truncateUtf16Safe(input, 30);
  expect(out.length).toBe(29);
  expect(out).toBe("a".repeat(29));
  expect(out.charCodeAt(out.length - 1)).toBeLessThan(0xd800);
});
```

If the source line is reverted to `meta.ttsText?.slice(0, 30)`, **the helper itself still passes** (helper-only proof) — the production-site change is what the 4 unit tests are guarding indirectly through the helper contract. The 4 unit tests exist primarily to lock the helper's surrogate-aware behavior at the exact 30-char boundary that the production site uses, so future regressions in the helper (e.g. someone "optimizing" it back to a raw `.slice`) are caught at test time.

**AFTER** (with the fix applied), the `onMessageSent` log line for the same input is surrogate-safe:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose \
  extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.utf16-truncation.test.ts

 ✓ gateway onMessageSent TTS preview UTF-16 truncation > drops a surrogate pair straddling the 30-char boundary (onMessageSent TTS path) 246ms
 ✓ gateway onMessageSent TTS preview UTF-16 truncation > pass-through for plain ASCII (no regression) 1ms
 ✓ gateway onMessageSent TTS preview UTF-16 truncation > emoji fully inside the 30-char window is preserved (no false-positive drops) 1ms
 ✓ gateway onMessageSent TTS preview UTF-16 truncation > undefined TTS falls back to empty string (the `meta.ttsText ?? ""` path is surrogate-safe) 0ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Duration  1.49s
```

## Evidence

- **Behavior addressed**: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts:64` now passes `meta.ttsText ?? ""` through `truncateUtf16Safe(..., 30)` before logging. Emoji straddling the 30-char boundary is dropped whole instead of emitting a lone high-surrogate half. ASCII input is unchanged. Undefined TTS text still logs as the empty string (preserved semantics).
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, repo checkout `/tmp/wt-98129a` (branch `fix/qqbot-gateway-onmessagesent-utf16`, live HEAD `cf5667f051`, base `openclaw/main @ 6cb82eaab8`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-98129a
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose \
    extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.utf16-truncation.test.ts
  # → 4/4 passed in 1.49s

  node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json \
    extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts \
    extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.utf16-truncation.test.ts
  # → EXIT=0

  pnpm tsgo:extensions
  # → silent — no type errors
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: vitest 4/4 passed; oxlint EXIT=0; `pnpm tsgo:extensions` silent.
- **Observed result after fix**: 2 files / +36/-1 LoC; 1 commit; source change at the exact `onMessageSent` log site; helper unit tests verify surrogate-aware behavior at the 30-char boundary. Body / HEAD consistency verified via `gh api graphql headRefOid` round-trip.
- **What was not tested**: Live integration with a running QQBot gateway that actually emits a 30-char-boundary emoji into the `onMessageSent` log. The unit tests verify the helper at the exact boundary the production site uses; the production site is a single-template-literal log line with the helper substituted in (no control flow changes). Live log capture is intentionally out of scope (matching the sibling PR pattern in #98058 / #98065).

## Diff scope

```
 extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts                            |  3 +-
 extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.utf16-truncation.test.ts      | 34 +++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 36 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

- Source: 2 net insertions in `gateway.ts` (1 import + 1 helper call replacing `.slice(0, 30)` with `??` fallback folded in).
- Tests: 34 net insertions in `gateway.utf16-truncation.test.ts` (NEW: 4 unit tests at the 30-char boundary).
- 0 already-landed files in this PR — clean branch from latest `openclaw/main @ 6cb82eaab8`.

## Security & Privacy

- No new network calls, no new persisted data, no new logging of secrets.
- The `truncateUtf16Safe` helper is canonical SDK surface, not a monkey-patch.
- The log line itself is unchanged in structure — only the truncation function backing it.

## Compatibility

- **Compatibility impact (minimal)**: log-line preview for ASCII input is byte-identical to before. Log-line preview for input where an emoji straddles the 30-char boundary is now surrogate-safe (no lone high surrogate). Backward-compatible for log scrapers that were already JSON-decoding the preview; fixes log scrapers that were failing on lone high surrogates.
- The `?.` optional-chain in the original code is folded into the `??` fallback inside the helper call (`meta.ttsText ?? ""`); the rendered log line for `meta.ttsText === undefined` is identical to before (the empty string).
- No config/env changes, no migration needed.
- Blast radius: 1 `onMessageSent` log line in 1 source file. No public API.

## What was not tested

- Live integration with a running QQBot gateway session. The fix is a single-helper substitution in an info log line; the helper's surrogate-aware behavior is unit-tested at the exact boundary the production site uses.
- The other `.slice(0, N)` sites in the qqbot gateway tree (message-queue command content preview, inbound-attachments STT transcript preview, stages/quote-stage refBody debug preview) are out of scope for this PR; they will be addressed in sibling split PRs (`#98129b`, `#98129c`, `#98129d`).

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? **Yes (small)** — log lines containing emoji at the 30-char boundary now have surrogate-safe previews instead of broken-glyph previews. Operators see a clean preview.
- Config/env/migration change? **No**.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? **No**.
- Backward compatibility: ASCII pass-through is unchanged; undefined `meta.ttsText` still renders as the empty string; emoji at boundary now drops whole (surrogate-safe) instead of emitting a lone high surrogate.

## Related

- Sibling UTF-16 truncation PRs in the same campaign:
  - #98008 twitch (utilizes canonical `truncateUtf16Safe`)
  - #97982 signal
  - #98058 msteams (closest reference pattern — 1 file, 3 call sites, inline test)
  - #98065 imessage (2 files, 2 call sites each, inline tests)
  - #98208 (this PR's streaming counterpart) — `fix(qqbot): drop dangling surrogates in streaming-c2c onDeliver preview`
- Plugin SDK source: `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-utility-runtime` exports `truncateUtf16Safe(input, maxLen)` and `sliceUtf16Safe(input, start, end)`.
- The `onMessageSent` callback is the public event hook that `sender.ts:228` invokes when a message is sent; the log line is captured by the gateway's info logger.

## AI disclosure

AI-assisted (Claude Sonnet); reviewed by human author before submission. Real environment verification (fresh vitest run on commit `cf5667f051`, oxlint, tsgo) performed by human author. Clean branch from latest `openclaw/main` per skill guidance on stale-base pitfalls.
